reasoning.issue: The question asks what test governs the Cumulative Amount (builder/available amount) basket and when it begins to accrue.
reasoning.analysis: The Cumulative Amount basket uses a 'greatest of' aggregation structure. The total basket equals (A) a starter amount (greater of $130M or 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA) PLUS (B) a sum of additive sources, where the core income-based component itself is determined on a 'greatest of' basis among three alternative tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since the start date, (2) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f) or other debt under Section 2.10(h)), and (3) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA. On top of these, additional additive sources include: 100% of equity proceeds not otherwise applied, retained asset sale proceeds (net cash proceeds retained after mandatory sweep obligations), investment returns, and debt-to-equity conversion amounts. The basket is therefore not a single-ratio test but rather a multi-source, greatest-of computation. There is no payment condition (e.g., no leverage ratio test) to use the basket itself — the Cumulative Amount is a capacity-building mechanism used as the cap for both RP (via Section 6.06) and RDP (via Section 6.09(a)(A)) payments, and also for investments (Section 6.03(x)). The basket starts accruing from 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs' per the start_date_language attribute.
reasoning.interactions: The builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) feeds the RDP builder basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)) via shares_with_rp_builder=true, meaning draws under both the RP and RDP covenants reduce the same Cumulative Amount pool. The basket is also reduced by investment usage (reduced_by_investment_usage=true) under Section 6.03(x). Asset sale retained proceeds flow into the builder via the proceeds_flow edge from asset_sale_event to 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket is based on a 'greatest of' three income/cash flow tests (50% CNI, Available Retained ECF, or 140% LTM EBITDA) plus a starter amount and several additive sources; it begins accruing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

stage2.answer: The Cumulative Amount basket is not a single-ratio test — it is computed as the sum of (i) a starter amount equal to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus (ii) the greatest of three alternative income/cash-flow measures: (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income, (b) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f) or other indebtedness under Section 2.10(h)), or (c) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus (iii) additional additive sources including 100% of equity proceeds not otherwise applied, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion amounts [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. The basket begins accruing from 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs' [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. Capacity is reduced dollar-for-dollar by usage under the RP covenant (Section 6.06), the RDP covenant (Section 6.09(a)(A)), and the investments covenant (Section 6.03(x)).

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted to dividend (distribute) equity interests it owns in Unrestricted Subsidiaries, and on what basis.
reasoning.analysis: Section 6.06(p) provides a categorical, uncapped permission for the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of an Unrestricted Subsidiary (as well as Indebtedness owed by an Unrestricted Subsidiary, its assets, and proceeds thereof). The norm's extracted v3 attributes confirm: is_categorical=true, capacity_category='categorical', no dollar cap, no grower percentage, and partial_applicability=false. No defeaters are listed against this norm. The permission expressly covers equity interests (covers_equity_interests=true) and proceeds thereof (covers_proceeds=true), and distributions can cascade (distributions_can_cascade=true). There is no condition_tree attached to this norm — it is a flat categorical carve-out from the general RP prohibition. The J.Crew blocker norm (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) is relevant only to the designation of Restricted Subsidiaries as Unrestricted Subsidiaries (it restricts designation where the entity would own Material IP), not to the distribution of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries. Separately, the unsub_designation entity confirms a $40M investment cap for designating subsidiaries as unrestricted, but that cap governs investments made in connection with designation, not downstream distributions of the resulting equity interests. No other defeaters or conditions are identified in the data.
reasoning.interactions: The unsub_distribution_basket (6.06(p)) has rebuilds_builder_basket=true, meaning distributions made under this basket rebuild the Cumulative Amount (builder basket), which is a beneficial interaction for the Borrower. No norm in the data defeats or conditions this permission. The J.Crew blocker (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) operates at the designation stage, not the distribution stage, and does not interact with 6.06(p).
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — Section 6.06(p) provides a categorical, uncapped permission to distribute by dividend the equity interests (and other assets/indebtedness) of Unrestricted Subsidiaries, with no conditions or defeaters applicable.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) [p. 198] expressly and categorically permits the Borrower to distribute, by dividend or otherwise, shares of Equity Interests of an Unrestricted Subsidiary (as well as indebtedness owed by such Unrestricted Subsidiary, its assets, and the proceeds thereof). This permission is uncapped, subject to no financial ratio condition, and has no defeaters in the data. As an additional benefit, distributions made under this basket rebuild the Cumulative Amount (builder basket). The J.Crew blocker [Section Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary, p. 89] is not implicated because it applies only at the designation stage, not to downstream distributions of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries.

citations.quote: the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof
no Restricted Subsidiary may be designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary if, immediately after giving effect to such designation, such Unrestricted Subsidiary would own or have an exclusive license

questions.gold_answer: Yes, under 6.06(p) the Borrower can dividend shares of Equity Interest or any assets of an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether investment baskets, restricted debt payment (RDP) baskets, or other baskets can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends.
reasoning.analysis:
Three cross-covenant reallocation mechanisms are present in the data:

1. **General RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) and General RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(I)) share the same dollar cap** — both are capped at the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. The general RP basket has `capacity_composition: 'fungible'` and `action_scope: 'reallocable'`; the general RDP basket has `action_scope: 'reallocable'` and is `general_purpose`. These baskets are cross-covenant fungible, meaning capacity used under one reduces availability under the other.

2. **Builder/Cumulative Amount basket (Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32)** — The Cumulative Amount (norm `6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission`) expressly covers make_dividend_payment, repurchase_equity, pay_subordinated_debt, AND make_investment in its scope. Critically, the builder basket is reduced by RP usage (`reduced_by_rp_usage: true`), RDP usage (`reduced_by_rdp_usage: true`), and investment usage (`reduced_by_investment_usage: true`). The builder RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)) expressly `shares_with_rp_builder: true`, confirming the Cumulative Amount is shared across RPs, RDPs, and investments.

3. **Investment basket 6.03(y)** — This investment pathway is explicitly sized as the greater of $130,000,000/100% EBITDA PLUS the aggregate of all amounts available under the RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)). This is a one-way incorporation: RDP capacity feeds investment capacity, not the reverse into RP capacity directly.

4. **Investment basket 6.03(x)** — investments up to the Cumulative Amount; this means investment capacity and RP/RDP capacity all draw from the same shared Cumulative Amount pool.

The general RP basket and general RDP basket are fungible/reallocable at the same $130M/100% EBITDA cap — using one reduces the other. The Cumulative Amount is expressly shared across RPs, RDPs, and investments (each use reduces it for all purposes). No standalone investment basket is shown to reallocate independently into the RP covenant without going through the Cumulative Amount shared pool.
reasoning.interactions: The builder basket (Cumulative Amount) is the central shared pool: `reduced_by_rp_usage`, `reduced_by_rdp_usage`, and `reduced_by_investment_usage` are all true, and the builder RDP basket (`6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission`) expressly `shares_with_rp_builder`. The general RP basket (6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (6.09(a)(I)) share the same dollar cap ($130M/100% EBITDA) with `capacity_composition: fungible` and `action_scope: reallocable`, creating a cross-covenant fungible pool. Investment pathway 6.03(y) incorporates RDP basket capacity into its investment sizing formula.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — there are two primary reallocation mechanisms: (1) the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(I)) share a single fungible $130M/100% EBITDA pool that can be used interchangeably for restricted payments or debt prepayments; and (2) the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is a shared pool expressly reduced by RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage, so capacity drawn for investments or RDPs reduces what is available for restricted payments and vice versa.

stage2.answer: Yes, there are two cross-covenant reallocation mechanisms. First, the general RP basket [Section 6.06(j), p. 196] and the general RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(I), p. 200] are fungible and reallocable — both are capped at the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period, and capacity used under either reduces the other. Second, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32] is a single shared pool expressly reduced by RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage, meaning amounts drawn for investments under Section 6.03(x) or for RDPs under Section 6.09(a)(A) reduce what remains available for restricted payments, and vice versa — the builder RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(A), p. 199] expressly shares this pool with the RP builder basket. Additionally, investment pathway Section 6.03(y) is sized partly by incorporating available RDP basket capacity, but that pathway flows into investment capacity rather than directly back into the RP covenant.

questions.gold_answer: Yes, under 6.06(j) amount available for Restricted Debt Payment under 6.09(a) and amounts available for Investments under 6.03(y) can be reallocated to the making of Dividends. 6.09(a) includes the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. 6.09(a) also includes other more tailored baskets available for Restricted Debt Payments which may or may not be available for reallocation, including intercompany debt payments and payments in connection with a reorganization or IPO.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividend payments under the restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis: Asset sale proceeds feed into dividends through two distinct channels. First, and most directly: norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds establishes that net cash proceeds from asset sales that are retained after applying the leverage-based sweep mechanics flow into the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) as clause (f). Specifically, only proceeds that are not required to be swept under Section 2.10(c) — i.e., proceeds retained because leverage is at or below 5.50x (0% sweep tier) or because amounts fall below de minimis thresholds — become available as a source of builder basket capacity. That builder basket capacity (norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) expressly permits dividend payments. The sweep tiers are: 100% sweep if First Lien Leverage Ratio > 5.75x; 50% sweep if > 5.50x but ≤ 5.75x; 0% sweep if ≤ 5.50x. De minimis exemptions also apply (individual: 0.3% of total assets; annual: greater of $40M and 0.3% of total assets). Second, asset sale proceeds that flow into the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j), norm 6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) or ratio basket (Section 6.06(o), norm 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) are fungible — those baskets are not proceeds-specific, but can be used regardless of the source of funds. However, the builder basket is the only basket that expressly incorporates retained asset sale proceeds as a capacity-building source. The general RP basket and ratio basket permit dividends independently of proceeds source.
reasoning.interactions: The retained asset sale proceeds norm (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds) contributes additively (child_index 6) to the builder usage permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission), which in turn scopes dividend payments. The proceeds_flow edge confirms that asset_sale_event proceeds flow to the builder source norm only when retained per sweep-tier exemptions or de minimis thresholds. The builder basket capacity is reduced by RP, RDP, and investment usage (reduced_by_rp_usage: true). No defeaters are present.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — asset sale proceeds that are retained after mandatory sweep obligations (i.e., not required to prepay term loans under Section 2.10(c)) accrete into the Cumulative Amount builder basket, which expressly permits dividend payments; additionally, dividends may be made under the general RP basket and ratio basket independent of proceeds source.

stage2.answer: Yes, asset sale proceeds can indirectly fund dividends through two pathways. First, net cash proceeds from asset sales that are retained after applying the leverage-based sweep tiers under Section 2.10(c) — i.e., the portion not required to prepay term loans — flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32], and that builder basket expressly permits dividend payments. Proceeds are fully retained (0% sweep) when First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.50x, 50% retained when between 5.50x and 5.75x, and fully swept (100%) when > 5.75x [Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage]; de minimis exemptions also apply (individual threshold: 0.3% of total assets; annual: greater of $40M and 0.3% of total assets) [Definition of Prepayable Net Cash Proceeds, p. 71]. Second, dividends may also be made under the general RP basket (greater of $130M / 100% of Consolidated EBITDA) [Section 6.06(j), p. 196] or, if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x on a pro forma basis, under the unlimited ratio basket [Section 6.06(o), p. 198] — both of which are fungible and not restricted by the source of funds used.

citations.quote: "Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):
"Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):

additional Dividends may be made, directly or indirectly, by any Group Member to Holdings and/or (without duplication) Holdings' direct or indirect equity holders in an aggregate amount not to exceed the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA
unlimited additional Dividends; provided that on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio...shall be no greater than the greater of (x) 5.75 to 1.00

questions.gold_answer: Yes, Retained Asset Sale Proceeds build the Cumulative Amount which consists of proceeds from: Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales not subject to prepayment on account of Section 2.10(c)(iv), permitting proceeds from any Asset Sale using the unlimited basket 6.05(z) if such Asset Sale is a sale of a product line and the pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.25x or less or if such test is no worse pro forma. Also includes asset sale proceeds not swept when First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 5.75x or less (50% of proceeds) or 5.50x or less (100% of proceeds). Also includes Net Cash Proceeds from non-collateral assets, ordinary course asset sales, asset sales from non-ratio baskets, casualty events, and proceeds from collateral assets below de minimis thresholds of $20M/15% EBITDA individual and $40M/30% EBITDA annual.

reasoning.issue: The question asks for the total amount of quantifiable dividend capacity across all applicable baskets under the restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis:
Per the Category N guidance, baskets must be classified and only general_purpose baskets are summed together; restricted_purpose, unlimited_conditional, and categorical baskets are listed separately.

**General-Purpose Baskets (summable):**
1. General RP Basket [Section 6.06(j)]: capacity_composition = 'fungible', cap = greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period. capacity_category = general_purpose.
2. Builder Basket / Cumulative Amount [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]: capacity_category = general_purpose, computed_from_sources. The Cumulative Amount is the greatest of: (A) the starter amount (greater of $130M or 100% LTM EBITDA), or (B) the three-test aggregate (greatest of 50% CNI, Available Retained ECF, or 140% LTM EBITDA), in each case plus equity proceeds (100%), retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion amounts. The floor starter is $130,000,000. No shares_capacity_pool link is identified between the General RP Basket and the Builder Basket, so they are independent pools — each with its own floor of $130M at minimum.
3. General RDP Basket [Section 6.09(a)(I)]: capacity_category = general_purpose, cap = greater of $130,000,000 or 100% LTM EBITDA. Note: this is a Restricted Debt Payment basket, not a dividend basket per se, but its capacity_category is general_purpose. However, the builder_rdp_basket shares with the RP builder basket (shares_with_rp_builder = true), so no double-counting there. The general_rdp_basket is a separate basket.

For purely quantifiable dollar floors:
- General RP Basket floor: $130,000,000
- Builder Basket floor (starter): $130,000,000 (the Cumulative Amount is at least greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA as a starter, then further augmented)

These two are independent (no shares_capacity_pool link), so the quantifiable dollar floor total for general_purpose dividend-applicable baskets = $130M + $130M = $260,000,000 minimum (each grower could expand to 100% LTM EBITDA if larger).

**Restricted-Purpose Baskets (listed separately, not summed into general total):**
- Management Equity Basket [Section 6.06(b)]: greater of $20,000,000 or 15% LTM EBITDA per annum (carry-forward permitted); restricted to equity repurchases for employees/officers/directors.

**Unlimited-Conditional Baskets (listed separately):**
- Ratio RP Basket [Section 6.06(o)]: unlimited, subject to pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x or no-worse test.

**Categorical Baskets (no fixed dollar cap, listed separately):**
- Tax Distribution Basket [Section 6.06(c)]: categorical, standalone-taxpayer limit.
- Holdco Overhead Basket [Section 6.06(k)]: categorical, covers admin/overhead/Public Company Costs.
- Equity Award Basket [Section 6.06(d)]: unlimited on condition (cashless exercise / tax withholding only).
- Unrestricted Subsidiary Distribution Basket [Section 6.06(p)]: categorical, distributions of unsub equity/assets/proceeds.
reasoning.interactions: The Builder RDP Basket [Section 6.09(a)(A)] shares capacity with the RP Builder Basket (shares_with_rp_builder = true), so usage of one reduces the other — they are not additive. The General RDP Basket [Section 6.09(a)(I)] is a separate general_purpose basket but scoped to debt payments, not dividends directly. The ratio RP basket [6.06(o)] is unlimited if the First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x (or no-worse) pro forma, making it the dominant basket when the ratio test is satisfied. No defeaters are present in the data.
reasoning.conclusion: The quantifiable dollar-floor dividend capacity from general_purpose baskets totals at minimum $260,000,000 ($130M General RP Basket + $130M Builder Basket starter floor, each independently expandable to 100% LTM Consolidated EBITDA if larger), plus a restricted-purpose management equity basket of up to $20M/year (greater of $20M or 15% EBITDA), plus unlimited capacity under the ratio basket if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma, and various categorical/unlimited-conditional baskets for specific purposes.

stage2.answer:
The total quantifiable dividend capacity, organized by category, is as follows:

**General-Purpose Baskets (independent pools, summable floor = $260,000,000):**
1. General RP Basket [Section 6.06(j), p.196]: greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period — available for dividends to Holdings and its equity holders with no ratio condition.
2. Builder Basket / Cumulative Amount [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]: begins at the greater of $130,000,000 or 100% LTM EBITDA (starter floor), then builds by the greatest of (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income, (b) Available Retained Excess Cash Flow, or (c) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus 100% of equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion amounts — each accruing since the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs; reduced by RP, RDP, and investment usage (but not by unsub designations). No shared-pool link exists between the Builder Basket and the General RP Basket, so they are independent; the combined quantifiable dollar floor is $130M + $130M = **$260,000,000** (expandable to the relevant EBITDA-based growers if larger).

**Restricted-Purpose Basket (not included in general total):**
3. Management Equity Basket [Section 6.06(b), p.195]: greater of $20,000,000 or 15% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA per annum (carry-forward permitted), restricted to repurchases of Qualified Capital Stock held by current/former officers, directors, consultants, or employees.

**Unlimited-Conditional Basket (no fixed dollar cap):**
4. Ratio RP Basket [Section 6.06(o), p.198]: unlimited dividends, subject to pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x (or, alternatively, the no-worse test: pro forma ratio no greater than the ratio immediately prior to the transaction without giving effect to the subject transactions).

**Categorical Baskets (purpose-limited, no fixed dollar cap):**
5. Tax Distribution Basket [Section 6.06(c), p.196]: tax distributions on a standalone-taxpayer basis for members of a consolidated tax group.
6. Holdco Overhead Basket [Section 6.06(k), p.197]: distributions for general corporate/administrative/overhead costs and Public Company Costs of Holdings or any direct/indirect parent.
7. Equity Award Basket [Section 6.06(d), p.196]: deemed repurchases of Equity Interests upon exercise of stock options for exercise price or tax withholding (cashless exercise / net settlement).
8. Unrestricted Subsidiary Distribution Basket [Section 6.06(p), p.198]: categorical permission to distribute equity interests, indebtedness, assets, or proceeds of an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division with negative EBITDA to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, examining all available RP baskets.
reasoning.analysis:
Three potential avenues exist for dividending the asset/business division to shareholders:

1. RATIO BASKET (Section 6.06(o)) — unlimited_conditional: Permits unlimited dividends if, on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the GREATER OF (x) 5.75x OR (y) the pre-transaction First Lien Leverage Ratio ('no-worse test'). At 6.0x pre-transaction, the absolute threshold (5.75x) is not met. However, the no-worse test is critical: dividending an asset with NEGATIVE EBITDA pro forma IMPROVES the consolidated EBITDA base (removing a drag), which REDUCES the First Lien Leverage Ratio. Therefore, the pro forma post-transaction leverage ratio will be LOWER than the pre-transaction 6.0x. Under the no-worse test, the permissible threshold is the pre-transaction ratio (6.0x), and the pro forma ratio will be below 6.0x. The no-worse prong is satisfied. Capacity: unlimited.

2. GENERAL RP BASKET (Section 6.06(j)) — general_purpose: Capped at the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. The asset is worth $200m, so this basket ($130m floor) is insufficient on its own to cover a $200m non-cash dividend unless EBITDA is above $200m (not indicated). This basket alone likely cannot accommodate the full $200m transaction.

3. BUILDER BASKET (Definition of Cumulative Amount) — general_purpose: Available but quantum depends on accumulated sources since closing date. Insufficient information to confirm availability at $200m.

4. UNSUB DISTRIBUTION BASKET (Section 6.06(p)) — categorical, but requires the asset to already be held by an Unrestricted Subsidiary. Not directly applicable unless a prior designation step is taken.

Conclusion on ratio basket: The no-worse test is the decisive path. Removing a negative-EBITDA business division improves consolidated EBITDA, lowering the pro forma leverage ratio below the pre-transaction 6.0x. The permissible threshold under the no-worse prong equals the pre-transaction ratio (6.0x), and the pro forma ratio will be strictly less than 6.0x. The condition is satisfied, and the ratio basket permits the dividend in unlimited amount.
reasoning.interactions: The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) operates as an OR condition: either (x) absolute 5.75x test OR (y) no-worse test. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) is independent general_purpose capacity but is capped at $130m floor. No defeaters are present. No shares_capacity_pool link connects the ratio basket to other baskets, so it stands alone as unlimited_conditional capacity. The builder basket is a separate general_purpose pool but depends on accumulated sources.
reasoning.conclusion: The Borrower can dividend the $200m negative-EBITDA asset/business division to shareholders at a 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio by satisfying the no-worse prong of the ratio basket under Section 6.06(o): because the divested business has negative EBITDA, removing it pro forma improves (reduces) the consolidated leverage ratio below the pre-transaction 6.0x level, thereby satisfying the no-worse test and unlocking unlimited dividend capacity.

stage2.answer: Yes, the Borrower can dividend the $200m asset/business division to shareholders at 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, relying on the unlimited ratio basket under Section 6.06(o) [p.198]. Although the absolute threshold of 5.75x is not met, Section 6.06(o) contains an OR condition: the basket is also available if the Pro Forma First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the pre-transaction ratio (the 'no-worse test'). Because the divested business carries negative EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group improves (increases) Consolidated EBITDA and thereby reduces the pro forma leverage ratio below the pre-transaction 6.0x; the no-worse prong is satisfied, and the basket permits the dividend in unlimited amount. The general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) [p.196] provides an independent but capped alternative (greater of $130m / 100% of Consolidated EBITDA), which would be insufficient to cover the full $200m value on its own unless Consolidated EBITDA exceeds $200m. No defeaters are present in the extracted data.
